behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about real STT model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation), so a stubbed transcript would hide true failures (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets STT output preservation itself, not a required multi-agentic chain outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting recording through the production STT API and assert transcript segments include correct speaker labels across turns for each attendee; fail if labels are dropped, merged, or inconsistent enough to break attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic parsing/mapping logic on transcript text; a local function/object test can catch extraction failures (MOCK-MISS says no required cross-module boundary in behavior text). Rejected Integration: behavior text focuses on parsing extraction, not inter-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures with explicit assignment utterances and assert exact structured outputs (`assignee`, `task_text`) including edge cases (multiple assignees, punctuation, quoted tasks).

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment to preserve assignment coverage in generated action items (LLM-DEP). Deterministic mocks can validate wiring but cannot verify whether the model actually includes every assigned attendee. Rejected Workflow: behavior text requires one quality gate on final summary output, not explicitly multiple agentic decision stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcripts with known attendee-task assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in the structured output; run multiple trials to detect omission variability.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says decisions are already identified; the check is deterministic routing to schema fields, which is a local logic boundary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no requirement here to validate model decision quality, only field routing.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision objects, test the routing function maps them into `decisions` and not narrative keys; include malformed/partial inputs to ensure misroutes are caught.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates model extraction/placement quality from transcript content; stubs would always pass placement expectations and miss real model misses (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text is a single output-quality requirement on summary content.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing explicit decisions through the live summarizer and assert each expected decision is present in `decisions` (not only narrative text), with tolerance rules for paraphrase but not omission.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires sequential multi-step agentic processing (per-segment transcription/summarization plus merge coherence), so MULTI-STEP applies. Rejected Integration/System: those can verify chunking/order mechanics but cannot validate coherence and quality across chained model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Process a real >60-minute recording end-to-end with production chunking settings; assert segment sequencing completes, merged output contains consolidated action items/decisions without cross-segment contradictions, and no segment is dropped.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with real transcription and summarization model calls; failures can emerge from cross-stage agentic handoff and sequencing (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP + REAL-INFRA). Rejected System: system-only scope is insufficient because model output/agentic stage interactions are part of pass/fail.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like job on a real meeting audio file through upload, STT, LLM summarization, and structured delivery; assert unattended completion, valid schema output, and stage-level artifacts proving automatic handoffs succeeded.
